mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during documented rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce a positive return frequency above 55% or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which translates to higher earnings expectations and positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during supply-constrained periods or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across multiple consecutive 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates above 0.3% monthly while the federal funds rate remains unchanged, markets price in imminent monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in greater than 60% of triggered instances or the inflation-rate interaction term shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and corporate revenue projections, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with equity returns with Rank IC below 0.02 or positive return frequency drops below 50% during confirmed expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth surpassing 2.0% confirms robust economic expansion and supports higher corporate profit margins, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above threshold yields negative forward returns in greater than 55% of cases or loses predictive significance in out-of-sample validation tests.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in unemployment signals a broad economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0 and UNRATE month-over-month change > 0
failure_condition: The dual deterioration pattern fails to predict negative returns with greater than 60% accuracy or the interaction effect vanishes in rolling window analysis.
